headline: Consumer Tech News (November 11-15): Apple Plans To Launch An AI Wall Tablet, Meta Plans To Introduce Ads On Threads & More
summary: The EU directed Apple to stop geo-blocking on its media services. Taylor Swift fans leave Twitter for Bluesky. Microsoft hints at a hand-held Xbox.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Tesla, Disney, Gold — And Hedge Funds Project Bitcoin To Hit $100K-$150K
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Tesla Owners Who Don't Like Musk Are Letting Bumper Stickers Do The Talking
summary: An entrepreneur created a business of making anti-Elon Musk stickers. Sales have taken off since Donald Trump won the 2024 election.

headline: Activist Hedge Fund ValueAct Boosts Meta Stake With $121M Bet, But CEO Morfit Supports Mark Zuckerberg's AI Vision: Here's What Investors Should Know (Updated)
Mike Tyson Vs. Jake Paul: Netflix's Explosive Boxing Event Ends With Paul's Victory
summary: Jake Paul defeated boxing legend Mike Tyson in Netflix's first live boxing event, marking a major step for the streaming giant's expansion into live sports content.

headline: Cathie Wood Backs Elon Musk's $2 Trillion DOGE Initiative, Says Tesla CEO's Access To 'Proprietary Data' And AI Gives Him An Edge
summary: Cathie Wood, CEO of ARK Invest, has publicly backed Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Elon Musk's appointment to lead the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).

headline: IBM, Disney And Other Large Advertisers Return To Elon Musk's X After A Year-Long Boycott: 'We Super Appreciate'
summary: Elon Musk expressed gratitude as major brands like IBM, Disney, Comcast, and Warner Bros. Discovery resumed advertising on X, following a year-long boycott over brand safety concerns.

headline: T-Mobile Hit By Chinese Hackers After Rivals AT&T, Verizon Suffer Breach: Report
summary: A major cyber-espionage operation linked to Chinese intelligence has reportedly infiltrated T-Mobile and other telecom networks.

headline: Elon Musk's xAI Seeks $6B At $50B Valuation To Acquire 100K Nvidia Chips For Memphis Data Center
summary: Elon Musk's xAI is reportedly in the process of raising up to $6 billion. The funds are intended for purchasing 100,000 Nvidia chips.
